SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution

Class TaskContext

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution.TaskContext
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class TaskContext extends Object implements AutoCloseable
Task-level execution context handed to a Java ExecutionPlan during execute.

Mirrors (a subset of) Rust DataFusion's TaskContext. Callers can read the session id, task id, session config, runtime environment, memory pool, and registered UDF/UDAF names, and derive new task contexts via withSessionConfig(SessionConfig) / withRuntime(RuntimeEnv).

The TaskContext passed into ExecutionPlan.execute is owned by the adapter and closed after execute returns; implementations should not retain the reference. In contrast, instances produced by with* methods are owned by the caller and must be closed.

Note on with* semantics: Rust's with_session_config / with_runtime consume self. The Java bindings deliberately do NOT close this — a new TaskContext is returned alongside the original so that the adapter-owned execute() parameter remains valid.

Example: 

public RecordBatchReader execute(int partition, TaskContext taskCtx, BufferAllocator allocator) {
    String sid = taskCtx.sessionId();
    Map<String, String> opts = taskCtx.sessionConfig().options();
    return new MyBatchReader(data);
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext

  • Method Details

    • fromNativeAddress

      public static TaskContext fromNativeAddress(long addr)
      Internal FFI entry point: adopt ownership of a raw DfTaskContext pointer. Not intended for user code.
      Parameters:
      addr - a non-zero raw pointer produced by the Rust execute() upcall
      Returns:
      a TaskContext that owns and will close the underlying DfTaskContext

    • sessionId

      public String sessionId()
      Session ID for the session that produced this task.

      See TaskContext class-level example.

      Returns:
      the session id
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::session_id

    • taskId

      public Optional<String> taskId()
      Task identifier, if one was assigned.

      Example: 

      Optional<String> tid = taskCtx.taskId();
tid.ifPresent(id -> log.info("task {}", id));
      Returns:
      the task id, or Optional.empty() if none
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::task_id

    • sessionConfig

      public SessionConfig sessionConfig()
      Snapshot of the session configuration.

      Example: 

      try (SessionConfig cfg = taskCtx.sessionConfig()) {
    String batch = cfg.options().get("datafusion.execution.batch_size");
}
      Returns:
      a new SessionConfig (must be closed)
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::session_config

    • runtimeEnv

      public RuntimeEnv runtimeEnv()
      Runtime environment used to execute this task.

      Example: 

      try (RuntimeEnv rt = taskCtx.runtimeEnv()) {
    // use rt
}
      Returns:
      a new RuntimeEnv handle (must be closed)
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::runtime_env

    • memoryPool

      public MemoryPool memoryPool()
      Memory pool associated with this task's runtime.

      Example: 

      try (MemoryPool pool = taskCtx.memoryPool()) {
    long bytes = pool.reserved();
}
      Returns:
      a new MemoryPool handle (must be closed)
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::memory_pool

    • scalarFunctions

      public Map<String,ScalarFunctionHandle> scalarFunctions()
      Registered scalar UDFs, keyed by name.

      Values are placeholder ScalarFunctionHandle records — only the key set is meaningful today. See ScalarFunctionHandle for the planned evolution.

      Example: 

      boolean hasAbs = taskCtx.scalarFunctions().containsKey("abs");
      Returns:
      map of scalar function names to placeholder handles
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::scalar_functions

    • aggregateFunctions

      public Map<String,AggregateFunctionHandle> aggregateFunctions()
      Registered aggregate UDFs, keyed by name.

      Values are placeholder AggregateFunctionHandle records — only the key set is meaningful today. See AggregateFunctionHandle for the planned evolution.

      Example: 

      boolean hasSum = taskCtx.aggregateFunctions().containsKey("sum");
      Returns:
      map of aggregate function names to placeholder handles
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::aggregate_functions

    • withSessionConfig

      public TaskContext withSessionConfig(SessionConfig config)
      Returns a new TaskContext with the given session config.

      Unlike Rust's consuming form, this is NOT closed. The returned instance is owned by the caller.

      Example: 

      try (TaskContext overridden = taskCtx.withSessionConfig(newCfg)) {
    // overridden reflects the new config
}
      Parameters:
      config - the new session config
      Returns:
      a new TaskContext (must be closed)
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::with_session_config

    • withRuntime

      public TaskContext withRuntime(RuntimeEnv runtime)
      Returns a new TaskContext with the given runtime environment.

      Unlike Rust's consuming form, this is NOT closed. The returned instance is owned by the caller.

      Example: 

      try (TaskContext overridden = taskCtx.withRuntime(newRuntime)) {
    // overridden uses newRuntime
}
      Parameters:
      runtime - the new runtime environment
      Returns:
      a new TaskContext (must be closed)
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TaskContext::with_runtime

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
